scope: Pass B full-surface expansion: every endpoint documented under airtable.com/developers/web/api/ (meta bases/tables/fields, records, comments, webhooks) is enumerated below. 5 GET list resources are streams (bases, tables, records, webhooks, comments — comments via the fan_out dialect over each table's own records); 12 dialect-expressible mutations are writes.json actions (records, tables, fields, comments, webhooks). Legacy was fully read-only (capabilities.write:false); this pass flips capabilities.write to true. Base/workspace-provisioning (create-base, requiring a workspaceId this connector's spec has no config surface for), webhook-operational sub-actions, and view-metadata endpoints remain excluded (see per-endpoint reasons).

excluded.reason: get-base-schema is the same operation as GET /v0/meta/bases/{baseId}/tables (fields are embedded per-table, no separate list-fields endpoint exists); not a separate resource from the tables stream

creating a base requires a workspaceId and the schema.bases:write scope; this connector's spec.json is scoped to an already-existing base_id/table_id, with no workspace config surface to hang a create_base write action off of
single-record detail; the records stream already covers the list data

bulk multi-record PATCH (up to 10 records/request); the single-record create_record/update_record write actions already cover create and update, one request per record, matching the engine's one-request-per-record write semantics (conventions.md §3)
bulk destructive multi-record PUT clears every unincluded cell value across up to 10 records in one call; update_record's PATCH already covers the non-destructive per-record update case
destructive update clears every unincluded cell value on the record; update_record's PATCH already covers the non-destructive case
bulk multi-record DELETE via records[] query params; delete_record's single-record DELETE already covers deletion, one request per record
toggles delivery-retry notification state on an existing webhook; a minor operational knob, not core reverse-ETL surface
extends a webhook's expiration window; an operational keep-alive action, not reverse-ETL record data
webhook delivery-payload inspection/debugging endpoint, not a syncable business-data record stream
POST-with-body alternative to the same GET list-records operation the records stream already covers, used only when the query string would exceed URL length limits
returns the authenticated user's own identity/scopes for token introspection, not syncable business data
view metadata detail is a UI-configuration sub-resource already embedded in the tables stream's views field
